signals.title: NZD/JPY — G10 Bilateral Theme + Geopolitical Risk
signals.text: NZD/JPY at 94.93 — NZD is the week's G10 leader (+1.432%) while JPY remains the laggard (-0.496%), creating a 1.927% spread. Despite the NZD outperformance, surging Brent oil prices and Iran-related geopolitical tensions introduce acute downside risk for the cross. JPY COT LF net short -104,231 represents a crowded carry trade vulnerable to unwind if VIX (18.8, +12.19%) continues to climb toward the 25.0 risk-off threshold. NZD/JPY carry at +150bp (RBNZ 2.5% vs BoJ 1.0%) is currently secondary to safe-haven flows.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Positioning + Vol + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.41 — Price holds above the 160.00 psychological level where BoJ intervention risk is historically elevated. Options market is pricing aggressive protection against a reversal, with 25d RR at -1.37 (moderate put skew) and a +3.6% Vol Risk Premium (IV 7.2% vs HV30 3.6%). COT LF net short -104,231 (29.1% long) confirms a crowded short-JPY position. The combination of extreme positioning and negative options skew suggests a high-conviction hedge against a sharp JPY recovery.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6984 — COT LF net long +24,098 (63.8% long) contradicts a strong negative Economic Surprise Index (net -13 misses), signaling speculative overextension in the face of deteriorating fundamentals. Options market aligns with the downside risk, showing an elevated +4.2% VRP (IV 11.6% vs HV30 7.4%) and a -0.74 put skew. Institutional longs are vulnerable to a liquidation event if US data continues to outperform (USD CESI +51).

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Tariff Risk
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4019 — The 252-day correlation between USD/CAD and WTI Oil has collapsed to -0.930 (z = -1.92σ), indicating the pair is no longer trading on energy fundamentals. Trump's headline regarding Canadian wildfire-related tariffs introduces a political risk premium that decouples CAD from the +4.57% surge in WTI. Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% (+150bp carry) supports the USD bid despite the oil rally. Watch for a break of 1.4100 to confirm tariff-driven CAD weakness.

signals.title: EUR/GBP — Retail Sentiment Extreme
signals.text: EUR/GBP retail sentiment at 88% long vs 12% short — retail positioning is at a contrarian extreme. This diverges from the institutional bias where EUR CESI (-16) shows persistent data misses compared to GBP (-6). Retail is fading the EUR weakness ahead of the July 23 ECB decision; a hawkish hold is required to prevent retail capitulation. Watch for a move toward 0.8400 to trigger stop-loss hunting of the retail long position.
